Introduce a platform abstraction layer so Squad works with Azure DevOps
(Work Items, PRs, Pipelines) in addition to GitHub (Issues, PRs, Actions).

Platform module (packages/squad-sdk/src/platform/):
- types.ts: PlatformType, WorkItem, PullRequest, PlatformAdapter interfaces
- detect.ts: Auto-detect platform from git remote URL (github/ado)
- github.ts: GitHubAdapter wrapping gh CLI
- azure-devops.ts: AzureDevOpsAdapter wrapping az CLI
- ralph-commands.ts: Platform-specific Ralph triage commands
- index.ts: Factory createPlatformAdapter() + barrel exports

Coordinator prompt:
- Add Platform Detection section to squad.agent.md
- ADO command mapping table and prerequisites

Tests (57 passing):
- Platform detection from various remote URLs
- GitHub remote parsing (owner/repo extraction)
- ADO remote parsing (org/project/repo extraction)
- WorkItem/PullRequest type shape validation
- Ralph command generation for both platforms
- Edge cases (case insensitivity, unknown platforms)

Docs:
- docs/features/azure-devops.md: User guide
- docs/specs/platform-adapter-prd.md: Design spec

* feat: ADO configurable work item type, area/iteration paths, cross-project support

Add AdoWorkItemConfig interface supporting enterprise ADO scenarios:
- defaultWorkItemType: configure Scenario, Bug, etc. (default: User Story)
- areaPath: route work items to specific team backlogs
- iterationPath: place work items in specific sprints

* fix: Ralph ADO config resolution — read ado section from .squad/config.json

Ralph's coordinator prompt now explicitly instructs the coordinator to:
1. Read .squad/config.json BEFORE running any ADO work item commands
2. Use ado.org/ado.project for work item queries (may differ from repo)

4. Use ado.defaultWorkItemType when creating work items

This fixes the issue where Ralph on ADO repos would try the repo name
as the ADO project (e.g. 'squad-ado-test') instead of the actual
configured work item project.

fix: use base64 data URIs instead of webview resource URIs for PNG assets

Webview resource URIs (vscode-resource:) have been unreliable for
new Image() loads in VS Code webviews — failures are silent and vary
by VS Code version/platform. After 5+ rounds of fixes (CORS retry,
GC prevention, crossOrigin removal), the fundamental issue persisted.

This change reads PNG files in the extension host, encodes them as
base64 data URIs (data:image/png;base64,...), and sends those through
postMessage instead of webview resource URIs. Data URIs:
- Are explicitly allowed by the CSP (img-src data:)
- Bypass the webview resource server entirely
- Work reliably with new Image().src in all browsers
- Match how pixel-agents (reference impl) handles assets

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(feature-svc): fix CORS and rate limiting middleware order

Critical bugs fixed:
- CORS middleware was running AFTER rate limiting, so 429 responses
  had no Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers. Browsers blocked these
  as network errors, showing 'Unable to connect to Feature Service'
- Rate limiter ran before auth, so user_id JWT claim was unavailable
  and Financial policy fell back to per-IP (all users shared 10 req/60s)
- Production .env had CORS__ALLOWEDORIGINS__0=https://www.aivalid.id
  which OVERRODE appsettings.json, blocking https://aivalid.id (no www)

Middleware order: CORS → Auth → Rate Limiting (was: Rate → CORS → Auth)
Rate limit: GET/HEAD on financial endpoints now use Global policy (100/60s)
Only POST/PUT/DELETE use Financial policy (30/60s per user)
Skip OPTIONS preflight from rate limiting entirely

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: ICC mismatch variants now produce output files

encodeImageWithICCProfile() returned nil when CGColorSpaceCreateWithICCData
rejected malformed/synthetic ICC data, causing 0 mismatch files to be generated.

Changes:
- Add fallback encoding in encodeImageWithICCProfile() — encodes image as-is
  when color space creation fails, ensuring output files are always created
- Rewrite encodeImageWithMismatchedProfile() strategies 0-1 to use named color
  spaces (Display P3, AdobeRGB 1998) for real gamut/gamma mismatch on sRGB content
- Strategies 2-3 (Lab abstract, truncated) use synthetic ICC headers that trigger
  the new fallback path
- Result: 32 mismatch files per run (was 0), total output 152 → 184 files

Tested: native ASAN+UBSAN build, 0 sanitizer errors, all 19 seeds produce
complete variant sets (base + no-ICC + mismatch across PNG/TIFF formats)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v3.6.0: Add --report mode with severity-sorted output

Phase 12: Report reorganization and severity classification

New features:
- --report mode: professional severity-sorted security report
  - Banner header with tool version, date/time, SHA-256, file metadata
  - Findings grouped by severity: CRITICAL → HIGH → MEDIUM → LOW → INFO
  - CWE category summary with finding counts
  - CVE cross-references from active findings

commit.message:
fix(set_pps_rate): correct CFG-VALSET key IDs, units, and locked params

The previous commit used wrong key IDs (group 0x51 = I2C config instead
of 0x05 = timepulse), wrong LEN_TP1 ID, and nanoseconds instead of µs.

Correct key IDs from u-blox M10 ICD / SparkFun GNSS library:
  0x40050002  CFG-TP-PERIOD_TP1        (U4, µs, unlocked)
  0x40050003  CFG-TP-PERIOD_LOCK_TP1  (U4, µs, locked to GNSS)
  0x40050004  CFG-TP-LEN_TP1          (U4, µs, unlocked)
  0x40050005  CFG-TP-LEN_LOCK_TP1     (U4, µs, locked to GNSS)
  0x10050007  CFG-TP-TP1_ENA          (U1, bool)
  0x1005000b  CFG-TP-POL_TP1          (U1, bool: 1 = rising)
  0x20050030  CFG-TP-PULSE_LENGTH_DEF (U1: 1 = absolute µs length)

Split into three ≤32-byte VALSET frames (period, length, enable/mode).
Revert public API from nanoseconds back to microseconds (µs matches ICD).
Update interrupt.rs call site accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[loop invariants] fix loop invariant with function calls in && chain (#3741)

## Description

Fixes #3735

Loop invariants of the form `__ESBMC_loop_invariant(A && f(x) && B)`
(function call in the middle of `&&`) were mis-handled: the generic
`remove_sideeffects` path turned the expression into short-circuit GOTO
and dropped guard `A` from the stored invariant, leading to wrong
verification results.

**Fix:**

1. **goto_sideeffects.cpp** — Special-case `__ESBMC_loop_invariant`:
flatten the top-level `&&`, run `remove_sideeffects` per conjunct,
rebuild the `and` chain. The full expression is preserved in the
LOOP_INVARIANT instruction.
2. **goto_loop_invariant.cpp** — Extract DECL/FUNCTION_CALL instructions
for invariant temporaries into a side-effects block and re-insert it
before each ASSERT/ASSUME so calls are re-evaluated after HAVOC.
3. Loops with no modified variables are no longer skipped; the base-case
ASSERT is still emitted to catch invalid invariants.

**Tests:** `github_3735` (all_cases.c: fun first/middle/last in `&&`),
`github_3735_fail` (expect FAILED), `github_3735_multi_fc` (two FCs in
`&&`).

commit.message:
fix: move useRuntimeConfig() into request interceptor (#62)

## Problem

Calling `useRuntimeConfig()` at the top level of `api/index.ts` caused
the following error on every route navigation:

```
Error: [nuxt] instance unavailable
    at useNuxtApp
    at useRuntimeConfig
```

The module is imported before any Nuxt app context exists (e.g. at
server startup), so the Nuxt instance is not yet available.

## Fix

Moved `useRuntimeConfig()` from module top-level into the axios request
interceptor. The interceptor only runs during actual HTTP requests, at
which point the Nuxt app context is always available.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add root cause analysis and InputThrottleHelper workaround

Complete disassembly-based root cause analysis of the InputStateManager.dll
crash (PopulateContactInFrame) that affects all WinUI 3 apps on touch input.

Includes InputThrottleHelper.cs — a Win32 window subclass workaround that
throttles WM_POINTER messages before they reach the WinUI 3 lifted input
stack, preventing the semaphore overload that triggers the crash.

References: microsoft/microsoft-ui-xaml#10929

- ModelSnapshot.Save/Load for streaming I/O
- ModelSnapshot.LoadMapped for memory-mapped I/O (zero-copy)

- BitNetEngine.SaveSnapshot/LoadSnapshot integration
- CLI save/load commands
